title: Preserve existing UX: _cardMap fingerprint, FLIP scope, IntersectionObservers
description: Adapt the existing _cardMap smart-diffing fingerprint, FLIP reorder animations, and IntersectionObservers to work with the new virtualized list (TASK-031, TASK-032).
acceptanceCriteria: _computeSettingsFingerprint() (existing) still triggers full repool/reset on barsInterval/startBar/maxCues/etc. change.
FLIP animations fire only for cards visible before AND after a reorder.
_sparkObserver, _mixObserver, _enterObserver are still .disconnect()-ed before reuse - no observer leaks.
Observers are attached only to currently visible nodes (not the off-screen pool).
Existing UX behaviors (multi-select, keyboard nav, hover sparkline lazy-load) work identically.

steps.details: On fingerprint change, call Virtualizer.detach() + reset _cardPool + Virtualizer.attach(). Full rebuild.

Before update(), capture .getBoundingClientRect() for currently visible nodes (in _visibleIndex). After update(), capture new rects. For nodes whose content_id is in both maps and changed position, run Element.animate({transform: oldDelta}, [{transform: newDelta}], {duration: 200}). Off-screen 'moves' are skipped.

When renderItem assigns a new content_id to a node: _sparkObserver.unobserve(oldNode would not work since it's the same node); just .observe(node) again - IntersectionObserver dedupes observations of the same target.
Per CLAUDE.md: if >30 cards exit at once (e.g., big filter), skip FLIP entirely (instant snap). Apply the same rule to virtualizer: large filter shrink -> no animation.
tests/web/virtualization.test.js: scroll through 1000 tracks; assert observer.observe call count is bounded (~40); assert FLIP fires only on visible reorders.

technicalNotes: IntersectionObserver.observe() is idempotent on the same target - safe to call multiple times.
FLIP via Element.animate is GPU-accelerated when only transform changes; avoid top/left.
